companies.challenges:
【Technical Barriers】Nscale's infrastructure is built on a tightly integrated Dell and NVIDIA stack. ASUS must not only match the performance of the latest Dell hardware but also demonstrate seamless integration with Nscale's existing management and orchestration software. The switching cost from a software and operations perspective is significant.
【Supply Chain Barriers】Dell's global supply chain and ability to deliver at scale is a major advantage. With Nscale procuring tens of thousands of servers, ASUS must provide strong assurances and a proven track record of its ability to meet high-volume demands for cutting-edge components, especially supply-constrained GPUs.
【Business Challenges】The Dell-Nscale relationship is a strategic alliance, likely involving favorable pricing, dedicated support, and joint marketing. ASUS needs to create a compelling counter-offer that goes beyond unit price, potentially including co-engineering resources, flexible financing, and a clear long-term partnership roadmap.
companies.entryPoint:
【archetype戰術】Position ASUS as the agile and high-performance partner for a rapidly growing "neocloud" leader. Emphasize customization, engineering collaboration, and superior performance-per-watt as key differentiators against the more standardized offerings of a larger incumbent like Dell.
【Short-term (6-12 months)】Focus on a "beachhead" strategy. Instead of a full replacement, target a new, specific workload or a smaller-scale deployment. Propose a proof-of-concept (POC) with a differentiated solution, such as an AMD-based GPU server (ESC4000A-E12), to showcase performance and introduce supplier diversity. Engage the office of the CTO (David Power) with compelling performance benchmarks.
【Mid-term (1-2 years)】Establish a joint innovation lab to explore next-generation cooling technologies (such as liquid-to-chip) and server designs optimized for Nscale's specific AI workloads. This demonstrates a long-term commitment beyond a simple supplier relationship.
【Long-term (2+ years)】Become a primary supplier for a significant portion of Nscale's server fleet. Expand the partnership to include joint go-to-market strategies for specific industry verticals, leveraging Nscale's infrastructure and ASUS's hardware expertise.

techDecisionMaker.commStrategy: Taking AI server performance benchmarking and NVIDIA certification as the starting point, demonstrate ASUS's technical strength in the GPU server field. companies.volume: Approx. 10,000-15,000 units/year (estimated, based on 500,000 GPUs, 8 GPUs per server, deployed in 2-3 phases)
companies.platform: The project will utilize high-performance chipsets, likely GPUs from NVIDIA's latest architectures (H100/H200/B200/GB200). The servers are expected to be high-density 4U or 8U chassis with 8 GPUs per server, based on a custom architecture designed in partnership with OXMIQ Labs. CPU choice will likely be between Intel Xeon and AMD EPYC.
companies.application: AI training and inference at massive scale, cloud hosting for global hyperscalers, frontier labs, and supporting India's sovereign AI initiatives.
companies.currentSuppliers: Unknown (Greenfield project). Likely to engage with major ODMs like Quanta, Wistron (Wiwynn), Supermicro, or major OEMs like Dell, HPE.
companies.asusModel: AI 8GPU: ESC N8-E11 (HGX H100), ESC NB8-E11 (Blackwell), ESC8000-E12; General Purpose 2U: RS720-E12, RS720A-E12
companies.channel: SI: Datacipher (www.datacipher.com); SI: Microland (www.microland.com); SI: Inspira Enterprise (www.inspiraenterprise.com)
companies.challenges:
【Archetype Tactics】AM Green is a 'neocloud' building a massive, specialized AI cloud. ASUS must compete with top-tier ODMs on co-development and customized, high-performance hardware at scale.
【Technical Barriers】The involvement of Raja Koduri's OXMIQ Labs implies a very high technical barrier. They are likely architecting the entire stack from the silicon up. ASUS must demonstrate superior server design, thermal engineering, and power efficiency, and be prepared for deep co-design.
【Supply Chain Barriers】Securing ~500,000 latest-generation GPUs over 2-3 years is a huge challenge. ASUS will compete with giant ODMs (Quanta, Wiwynn, Supermicro) who have stronger relationships with NVIDIA and operate at a larger scale.
【Business Challenges】This multi-billion dollar deal will be hyper-competitive on price. ASUS has no existing relationship and will need an extremely aggressive commercial strategy to break in against established ODMs.
companies.entryPoint:
【Archetype Tactics】Engage as a technology partner, not just a vendor. Focus on ASUS's engineering prowess and flexibility. Build a relationship with OXMIQ Labs to position ASUS as the ideal hardware partner.
【Short-term (6-12 months)】1. High-Level Engagement with Anil Chalamalasetty, Mahesh Kolli (AM Green), and Raja Koduri (OXMIQ). 2. Showcase advanced GPU server platforms (e.g., ESC NB8-E11, ESC8000-E12) and provide evaluation units. 3. Arrange technical deep-dive workshops with OXMIQ and AM Intelligence Labs.
【Mid-term (1-2 years)】1. Propose a semi-custom or fully-custom server design. 2. Secure a pilot project for a PoC cluster or ancillary systems. 3. Provide a detailed supply chain plan demonstrating volume and pace capabilities.
【Long-term (2-3 years)】1. Become a primary server supplier for the 1GW data center. 2. Evolve into a long-term technology partner for future designs.
【Key Actions】1. Assign a senior executive sponsor. 2. Form a dedicated tiger team (engineering, sales, supply chain). 3. Leverage ASUS's local presence in India.

companies.challenges: 【Financial Industry Tactics】【Technical Requirements】Murata Manufacturing has clear requirements for server quality and technical support, and needs to provide complete technical documents and after-sales service commitments. 【Supply Chain Barriers】The current main suppliers are Dell, HPE, Fujitsu, which have established stable cooperative relationships and service systems. ASUS needs to demonstrate clear differentiated value to win replacement or new opportunities. 【Market Characteristics】The Japanese market prefers local brands and long-term cooperative relationships. New suppliers need to invest time to build trust and provide Japanese technical support. 【Industry Characteristics】The financial industry has extremely high requirements for system stability, information security compliance (such as PCI DSS), and disaster recovery. Server suppliers need to provide complete security certification and SLA guarantees.

companies.platform: NVIDIA RTX 4080 GPU server (current), RTX 5080 GPU server (planned). 1U-2U gaming GPU server architecture, each server supports 8-16 concurrent game streams
companies.application: GeForce NOW cloud gaming streaming service (NVIDIA official alliance partner), supports 2,200+ games, 4K/120FPS. Additional applications: CG rendering, computing power rental for animation studios
companies.currentSuppliers: Unconfirmed (presumably an ODM/OEM supplier cooperating with NVIDIA)
companies.asusModel: Cloud Gaming GPU: ESC4000A-E12 (AMD GPU), ESC8000-E12 (4U 8-GPU); General Purpose Server: RS720-E12 (2U), RS700-E11 (1U)
companies.channel: SI/DIST: Telehouse Thailand (Data Center Partner); Com7 (Retail Channel)
companies.challenges: 【NeoCloud/AI Startup Tactics】【Technical Barriers】Brother Pictures uses NVIDIA GeForce RTX gaming-grade GPUs (not enterprise-grade HGX), and the server specifications are different from traditional AI servers, requiring solutions optimized for cloud gaming streaming. 【Supply Chain Barriers】As an NVIDIA GeForce NOW Alliance Partner, they may be influenced by NVIDIA's recommended suppliers, but it is not a mandatory binding. The investment scale of 160 million Thai baht (approximately 4.5 million USD) is relatively small. 【Business Challenges】The company is small, and procurement decisions are concentrated in the CEO, requiring the establishment of direct high-level relationships. The budget is limited, and there is high sensitivity to TCO. 【Industry Characteristics】The cloud gaming market is growing rapidly, and Thailand is positioned as a cloud gaming hub in Southeast Asia (covering CLMV countries), with great potential for future expansion.
companies.entryPoint: 【NeoCloud/AI Startup Tactics】【Short-term (6-12 months)】1. Establish contact through the KDDI parent company relationship of Telehouse Thailand, as ASUS and KDDI already have a cooperative foundation in Japan. 2. Provide POC testing of ASUS gaming GPU servers to demonstrate performance and stability advantages in GeForce NOW streaming scenarios. 3. Provide pre-configured GPU server solutions for the RTX 5080 upgrade demand. 【Mid-term (1-2 years)】4. Support Brother Pictures in expanding to the CLMV market (Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar, Vietnam) and provide regional deployment solutions. 5. Provide multi-purpose server solutions for CG rendering and AI inference to help them expand their non-gaming business. 【Long-term (2-3 years)】6. Become their core server supplier as the user base grows from 300,000 to the million level. 7. Jointly develop customized server solutions for the Southeast Asian cloud gaming market. 【Key Actions】Arrange a technical exchange meeting with CEO Alan Archapiraj through the KDDI/Telehouse relationship.

【Technical Barrier】RunSun Cloud focuses on the latest NVIDIA Blackwell and Grace Hopper platforms, especially the liquid-cooled GB200 NVL72. ASUS needs to demonstrate that it has a competitive and readily available solution; any delay in product launch will be a major disadvantage.

ASUS needs to prove it has superior supply chain capabilities to ensure a stable supply of these high-demand key components for RunSun.
【Business Challenge】As a startup, RunSun is likely very price-sensitive and may have already secured preferential pricing from its initial suppliers. ASUS needs to adopt an aggressive pricing strategy and offer flexible business terms. Additionally, their partnership with system integrator NetClass adds complexity to the sales process.
